questions.question: What does entity linking add on top of NER?
options: It maps each detected mention to a unique entry in a knowledge base (Wikidata, Wikipedia, or a domain KB)
questions.explanation: EL turns a mention into a canonical KB id, disambiguating between same-name entities.

questions.question: What are the two main subtasks in entity linking?
options: Candidate generation (shortlist of plausible KB entries) and disambiguation (pick the right one given context)
questions.explanation: EL decomposes into proposing candidates then ranking them by contextual fit.

questions.question: Why must mention recall be reported alongside disambiguation accuracy?
options: Mention recall is mandatory by GDPR
Disambiguation cannot recover from missing candidates; the pipeline is bounded by candidate-generation recall
questions.explanation: If candidates miss the gold entity, no disambiguator can fix it; recall floors pipeline quality.

questions.question: How does GENRE perform entity linking?
options: Decodes the entity's canonical name token-by-token under constrained decoding over a trie of valid KB ids
questions.explanation: GENRE generates the canonical KB name with constrained decoding to guarantee a valid id.

questions.question: What is NIL handling in entity linking?
options: Predicting a 'not in KB' label when no candidate is a real match (emerging entities, obscure people)
questions.explanation: NIL prediction prevents guessing wrong KB ids for entities the KB does not cover.

questions.question: Why does popularity bias hurt entity linking in specialized domains?
options: Models trained on web data over-predict frequent entities (e.g. basketball Jordan over the ML researcher Michael I. Jordan)
It biases toward older entities only
questions.explanation: Popularity priors skew predictions away from less-common, domain-specific name-clashes.

questions.question: What is a safe LLM-EL pattern in 2026?
options: Provide a candidate list and use constrained JSON output that the LLM can only choose from valid KB ids
questions.explanation: Constraining the LLM to a valid candidate list prevents made-up KB ids and keeps output queryable.

questions.question: Why must NER mention boundaries be exact for entity linking to work?
options: Boundary errors propagate: 'Bank of America' clipped to 'Bank' surfaces wrong candidates and tanks EL recall
Boundary precision only affects NER metrics, not entity-linking candidate quality
questions.explanation: Mis-bounded mentions retrieve the wrong alias set, propagating errors into disambiguation.
